rationale: The thread never identifies a root cause — the reporter just says it stopped reproducing after an update and the maintainer closes with 'can reopen if needed,' leaving the diagnosis genuinely open. X fabricates a specific, confident root-cause story (chunked-prefill splitting video tokens, a stage_config.py max_num_batched_tokens fix) that appears nowhere in the ground truth and presents it as verified fact — exactly the 'confident wrong answer' pattern that should score low. Y instead preserves the thread's actual epistemic state, explicitly stating uncertainty about whether video features dropped or wording just missed keywords, mirrors the maintainer's real ask for printed text/audio output, and closes with the same reopen condition as the actual resolution, even though it also speculates loosely about an unconfirmed fix.
